questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The thin metallic strip of vernier calipers moves downward from top to bottom in such a way that it just touches the surface of beaker. Main scale reading of caliper is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> whereas its vernier constant is 0.1 <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mm</mi></math>. The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>4</mn><mtext>th</mtext></msup></math> of vernier scale division is coinciding with main scale division. The actual depth of beaker in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mm</mi></math> is (when zero of vernier coincides with zero of main scale),
questions.solution:
We are measuring the depth from metallic strip of vernier calipers.

Given,

The least count of vernier calipers LS=0.1 mm=0.01 cm

Main scale reading MSR=6.4 cm

Vernier scale reading VSR=4

So the reading of the depth,D=MSR+(VSR×LC)=6.4+(4×0.01)=6.44 cm

Because, there is no zero error, so depth will be same as above.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For a screw gauge least count of the main scale is <math><mn>1</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Find the minimum number of divisions on the circular scale so that it can measure a diameter of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>μm</mi></math>
questions.solution:
Least count of screw gauge =5 μm

L.C=pitchno. of div on circular  scale

5 μm=1 mmN

N=200
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A cooling curve is plotted between the temperature of a hot body and time. Which of the following is not true for the cooling curve?
options: Cooling is faster from a calorimeter having a larger surface area than a smaller one.
Cooling is faster from a calorimeter painted black from outside than that from a polished surfaced calorimeter.
Cooling is faster from a copper calorimeter than from a steel calorimeter.
The rate of cooling remains same throughout the experiment. Hence the graph is a straight line.
questions.solution: The rate of cooling is faster at the beginning and then decreases and becomes constant.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two resistance $\mathrm{X}$ and $\mathrm{Y}$ are connected in the two gaps of a meterbridge and the null points is obtained at $20 \mathrm{~cm}$ from zero end. When the resistance of $20 \Omega$ is connected in series with the smaller of the two resistance $\mathrm{X}$ and $\mathrm{Y}$, the null point shifts to $40 \mathrm{~cm}$ from left end. The value of smaller resistance in ohm is
questions.solution:
For a meterbridge, $\frac{\mathrm{X}}{\mathrm{Y}}=\frac{l}{100-l}$
In the first case, $l=20 \mathrm{~cm}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{\mathrm{X}}{\mathrm{Y}} & =\frac{20}{100-20}=\frac{20}{80}=\frac{1}{4} \\
\therefore \quad 4 \mathrm{X} & =\mathrm{Y}
\end{aligned}
$$

In the second case, $l^{\prime}=40 \mathrm{~cm}$
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & \frac{\mathrm{X}^{\prime}}{\mathrm{Y}^{\prime}}=\frac{40}{100-40}= \\
& \text { But, } \mathrm{X}^{\prime}=\mathrm{X}+2 \\
\therefore \quad & \frac{\mathrm{X}+20}{\mathrm{Y}}=\frac{2}{3} \\
\therefore \quad & \frac{\mathrm{X}+20}{4 \mathrm{X}}=\frac{2}{3} \\
\therefore \quad & 8 \mathrm{X}=3 \mathrm{X}+60 \\
\therefore \quad & \mathrm{X}=12 \Omega
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A galvanometer, with a scale divided into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>150</mn></math> equal divisions, has current sensitivity of <math><mn>10</mn></math> divisions per milliampere and voltage sensitivity of <math><mn>2</mn></math> divisions per millivolt. Find shunt resistance for making an ammeter of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Ig=15 mAVg=75 mVI=6 A⇒ Rg=VgIg=5 Ω
S = IgRgI–Ig=12.5 × 10–3 Ω
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The jaws of a vernier calipers touch the inner wall of calorimeter without any undue pressure. The position of zero of vernier scale on the main scale reads 3.48. The  6th of vernier scale division is coinciding with any main scale division. Vernier constant of calipers is 0.01 cm. Find actual internal diameter of calorimeter, when it is observed that the vernier scale has a zero error of -0.03 cm.
questions.solution:
The jaws of a vernier calipers touch the inner wall means we are measuring inner radius.

Given,

The main scale reading MSR=3.48 cm

The Least count of vernier calipers LC=0.01 cm

The vernier scale reading VSR=6

Diameter of the calorimeter measured is, 

D=MSR+VSR×LC

D=3.48+0.01×6=3.54 cm

There is a negative zero error of -0.03 cm. So, the actual diameter of the calorimeter will be, 

D'=D-zero error=3.54--0.03=3.57 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The figure shows two diagrams of the same screw gauge. In the first case, nothing is kept in its jaw and in the second case a small ball is kept between the jaws for which the diameter is required to be measured. The number of circular divisions on the shown screw gauge is 50. It moves 0.5 mm on the main scale for one complete rotation and the main scale has 12 mm marks. The diameter of the ball is
questions.solution:
Pitch = 0.5 mm

L.C = 0.5 50

= 0.01 mm

M.S.R = 2

Neck reading  = 25

d = 2 × 0.5 + 25 × 0.01

= 1.25 mm

Considering zero error

Here zero error is

= + 5 × 0.01

= + 0.05 mm

so actual diameter

= d - 0.05

= 1.20 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The length of an object is measured using a vernier system whose main scale is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> long with <math><mn>600</mn></math> divisions. If <math><mn>19</mn></math> divisions of the main scale coincide with <math><mn>20</mn></math> divisions of the vernier scale, then its least count is
questions.solution:
∵ Least count =MSD-VSD

∵ MSD=30100600=5×10-4 m

and  VSD=1920×MSD

=1920×5×10-4=4.75×10-4

∴ Least count =5-4.75×10-4=0.025 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A screw gauge with a pitch of <math><mn>0.5 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mtext>mm</mtext><mtext></mtext></math> and a circular scale with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> divisions is used to measure the thickness of a thin sheet of aluminum. Before starting the measurement, it is found that when the two jaws of the screw gauge are brought in contact, the <math><msup><mrow><mn>45</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> division coincides with the main scale line and that the zero of the main scale is barely visible. What is the thickness (in <math><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>) of the sheet if the main scale reading is <math><mn>0.5 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mtext>mm</mtext></math> and the <math><msup><mrow><mn>30</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math> division coincides with the main scale line? If thickness in mm is K, mark 100K as answer
questions.solution:
Least count, LC=0.550=0.01 mm

Zero error, ZE=50-45×0.01=-0.05 mm

Main Scale Reading MSR=0.5 mm
Thickness, t=0.5 mm +30×0.01 mm - -0.05 mm

t=0.85 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a meter bridge experiment, resistances are connected as shown in figure. The balancing length l1= 55 cm . Now, an unknown resistance x is connected in series with P and the new balancing length is found to be 75 cm . What is the value of The value of 11x (in ohm)?(Given, P=4 Ω )
questions.solution:
Given in the meter bridge,

In I st case, PQ=l1l2

⇒4Q=55100-55

⇒Q=4×4555 ...(i)

In 2 nd case, P+xQ=7525

⇒4+x3=Q ...(ii)

From Equations (i) and (ii), we get

4+x 3 = 36 11

4+x= 108 11

x= 108−44 11

=6411

⇒11x=64
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A student measures the distance covered and time taken by a body under free fall which was initially at rest. He uses this data for estimating <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math>, the acceleration due to gravity. If the maximum percentage errors in measurement of the distance and the time are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> respectively, then the maximum possible percentage error in the estimation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
From the relation

h = ut + 1 2 gt 2

h = 1 2 gt 2 ⇒ g = 2 h t 2 ∵ body initially at rest

Taking natural logarithms on both sides, we get

lng=lnh-2lnt+ln2

Differentiating, Δ g g = Δ h h - 2 Δ t t

For maximum permissible errors, we add both the errors. So

Δgg×100max=Δhh×100+2×Δtt×100


Δ h h × 100 = e 1 and Δ t t × 100 = e 2

Therefore, Δ g g × 100 max =  e 1 + 2 e 2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two full turns of the circular scale of a screw gauge having <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> divisions cover a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. Further, it is found that the screw gauge has a zero error of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>02</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. While measuring the diameter of a thin wire, a student notes the main scale reading of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> and the number of circular scale divisions in line with the main scale as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>37</mn></math>. The diameter of the wire is,
questions.solution:
Distance cover in 1 full turn will be 1 mm2=0.5 mm.

Least count of screw gauge,

LC=Distance cover in 1 full turnnumber of division in circular scale=0.5 mm50=0.01 mm

Reading of the screw gauge,R= main scale reading+Circular scale reading×LC

R=4 mm+37×0.01 mm=4.37 mm

Reading without zero error, R'=R-zero error=4.37 mm--0.02 mm=4.39 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a Searle's experiment, the diameter of the wire as measured by a screw gauge is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>050</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. The pitch of screw gauge is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> and number of divisions on circular scale is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn></math>. The length, measured by a scale of least count <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>, is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. When a weight of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> is suspended from the wire, the elongation is measured to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>125</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> by a micrometer of least count <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>001</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. Find the maximum percentage error in the measurement of Young's modulus of the material of the wire from these data. Round off answer to nearest integer
questions.solution:
First, recall the formula of Young's modulus in terms of force applied, cross- sectional area of the wire and strain produced,

y=mgAlΔl=mgπr2lΔl,

So, the percentage error in Young's modulus,

Δyy×100=2Δrr+Δll+ΔΔlΔl×100

=2×0.0010.05+0.1100+0.0010.125×100

=250+11000+1125×100

=0.04+0.001+0.008×100=4.90.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In an experiment on the specific heat of a metal, a 0.20 kg block of the metal at 150°C is dropped in a copper calorimeter (of water equivalent 0.025 kg) containing 150 cm<sup>3</sup> of water at 27°C. The final temperature is 40°C. The specific heat of the metal is -
questions.solution:
Heat lost by the metal = Heat gain by water + calorimeter

0.20×103×c×150-40=175×13

c=175×13110×200≈0.1 cal g-1 °C-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A vernier calliper has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> divisions on the vernier scale which coincide with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>19</mn></math> divisions on the main scale. The least count of the instrument is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The length of one main scale division is
questions.solution:
20 division on vernier scale = 19 division on the main scale

20 VRS = 19 MSR

L.C = MSR - VSR = 0.1 mm

⇒MSR-1920MSR=0.1 mm

⇒MSR=2 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A galvanometer of resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is connected to a battery of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> along with a resistance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3000</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math>. In this case, a full-scale deflection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi>units</mi></math> is obtained in the galvanometer. In order to reduce this deflection to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>units</mi></math>, how much more resistance (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math>) should be added to the circuit in series?
questions.solution:
The deflection in a galvanometer is directly proportional to the current.

First case:

30x=225+3000

Second case:

10x=225+3000+R

⇒3=25+3000+R25+3000

On solving, we get

R=6050 Ω
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In an experiment for the determination of the speed of sound in air using the resonance method, the length of the air column that resonates in the fundamental mode with a tuning fork is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. When this length is changed to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>35</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>, the same tuning fork resonates with the first overtone. The end correction is
questions.solution:
λ4=e+l1

3λ4=e+l2

e+l2=3e+3l1

e⁡=l⁡2-3l⁡12=0.35-0.32=0.052=0.025 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The main scale of a vernier calliper reads in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mm</mi></math> and its vernier scale is divided into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> divisions, which coincides with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> divisions of the main scale. It was also observed that, when the two jaws are brought in contact, the zero of the vernier scale coincided with the zero of the main scale. The edge length of a cube is measured using this vernier calliper. The main scale reads <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> and the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mi>nd</mi></msup></math> division of the vernier scale coincides with the main scale. The edge length (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mm</mi></math>) of the cube is K, then find 100K
questions.solution:
8 VSD = 7 MSD

⇒1 VSD = 78 MSD

LC = 1 MSD - 1 VSD

LC = 1 MSD - 78 MSD = 18 MSD = 18 mm

l = 12 + 2×18=12.25 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The distance moved by the screw of a screw gauge is <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> in four rotations and there are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> divisions on its cap. When nothing is put between its jaws, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>30</mn><mi>th</mi></msup></math> division of circular scale coincides with the reference line, with zero of circular scale lying above the reference line. When a plate is placed between the jaws, the main scale reads <math><mn>2</mn></math> divisions and the circular scale reads <math><mn>20</mn></math> divisions. The thickness of the plate is
questions.solution:
Pitch = 1 2  mm
least count = 0.5 mm 50 =0.01 mm
Reading =[ 2×  1 2  +20×0.01 −( −30×0.01 ) ]=1.5 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A battery of emf <math><mn>12</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> and internal resistance <math><mn>2</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is connected in series with a tangent galvanometer of resistance <math><mn>4</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The deflection is <math><msup><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup></math> when the plane of the coil is along the magnetic meridian. To get a deflection of <math><msup><mrow><mn>30</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>o</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> the resistance to be connected in series with the tangent galvanometer is
questions.solution:
I=ktan⁡θ,k being reduction factor and I∝1R

⇒  R+rR+r+R′=tan⁡θ′tan⁡θ

⇒   4+26+R′=tan⁡30otan⁡60o

⇒  R′=12 Ω
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A convex lens forms inverted image of a real object on a fixed screen. The size of image is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. When lens is displaced <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> along principle axis it again forms a real image of size <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> on the screen. Focal length of the lens is .
questions.solution:
Since      m1 m2=1   ⇒   h1h0 h2h0=1

h0= h1 h2=6cm

for first position of the lens

m=-32=- x + 40x

⇒   3x=2x+80

⇒   x= 80

u=-x, y=(40+x)

1f=1120-1-80

⇒f=48 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The figure shown below is a screwgauge. When the circular scale division matches the main scale at  43, the 6.5 mm mark on the main scale is just visible. The main scale has 1 2  mm marks. In complete rotation, the screw advances by 1 2  mm and circular scale has 50 divisions. The reading of the screwgauge is
options: 6.93 mm
5.93 mm
4.93 mm
7.93 mm
questions.solution: Object thickness <math> <mrow> <mo>=</mo> <mo>6.5</mo> <mtext>mm</mtext> <mo>+</mo> <mn>4</mn> <mn>3</mn> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mo>/</mo> <mn>2</mn> <mo>mm</mo> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>5</mn> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>=</mo> <mtext>6.93</mtext> <mo>mm</mo> </mrow> </math>
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In an experiment on measurements of emf of a cell by a potentiometer, the balancing length for a cell of emf <math><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> and internal resistance <math><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math> is found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math>. Now if another cell of emf <math><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></math> and internal resistance <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math> is connected in parallel to the first cell and balancing length determined, then the balancing length will be-
questions.solution:
Since potentiometer doesn't draw any current to measure the emf of the cell, the balancing length will remain the same.

balancing length =l
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>N</mi></math> divisions on the main scale of a vernier calipers coincide with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>N</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> divisions of the vernier scale. If each division of main scale is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> units, then the least count of the calipers is
questions.solution:
It is given that N+1 Divisions on vernier scaleV.S.D.=N Divisions on main scale M.S.D.
⇒ 1 V.S.D.=NN+1M.S.D.

Least count of vernier callipers L.C.=1 M.S.D.-1 V.S.D.

⇒  L.C.=a-aNN+1=aN+1 unit
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A student measured the length of a rod and wrote it as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. Which instrument did he use to measure it?
options: A vernier calliper where the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> divisions in vernier scale matches with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> division in main scale and main scale has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> divisions in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>.

A screw gauge having <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> divisions in the circular scale and pitch as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>.

A screw gauge having <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> divisions in the circular scale and pitch as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
As per the given data the vernier has a least count of 0.01 cm, which is consistent with the information given in the question.

The measured value of the length of the rod=3.50 cm

So, the least count of the measuring instrument must be

0.01 cm=0.1 mm

For the vernier scale

10 MSD=1 cm=10 mm

→1MSD=1 mm

also 9 MSD=10 VSD

∴ Least count (L.C.)

=1 MSD-1 VSD

=(1-0.9) mm

=0.1 mm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: There are two vernier calipers both of which have 1 cm divided into 10 equal divisions on the main scale. The Vernier scale of one of the calipers C1 has 10 equal divisions that correspond to 9 main scale divisions. The Vernier scale of the other caliper C2 has 10 equal divisions that correspond to 11 main scale divisions. The readings of the two calipers are shown in the figure. The measured values (in cm) by calipers C1 and C2 respectively, are
questions.solution:
For caliper C1

10 VSD=9 MSD

LC=1 MSD-1 VSD

LC=0.01 cm

Measured value=Main scale reading+vernier scale reading×least count

=2.8+7×0.01 cm

=2.87 cm

For caliper C2

10 VSD=11 MSD

LC=-0.01 cm (Negative least count means we have to take VSR from the right of the scale

Measured value =2.8+10-7×0.01 cm

=2.83 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The main scale of a vernier caliper reads <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> divisions. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> divisions of vernier scale coincide with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> divisions of the main scale. When the two jaws of the calipers touch each other, the fifth division of the vernier coincides with a main scale division and the zero of the vernier is to the right of zero of the main scale. When a cylinder is tightly placed between the two jaws, the zero of vernier scale lies slightly to the left of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3.2 </mn><mi>cm</mi></math> and the fourth vernier division coincides with the main scale division. The diameter of the cylinder is,
questions.solution:
LC=110 mm=0.01 cm

and e=VSD×LC=0.05 cm

Therefore,

TSR=MSR+(VSD×LC)-e

⇒TSR=3.1+(4×0.01)-0.05

⇒TSR=3.14-0.05=3.09 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If a tuning fork of frequency (f0) 340 Hz and tolerance ±1% is used in the resonance column method for determining the speed of sound. If the first and the second resonance are measured at l⁡ 1 = 2 4.0 cm and l⁡ 2 = 74.70 cm, then the permissible error in speed of sound is
questions.solution:
V = 2 f 0 l 2 - l 1

let l 2 - l 1 = x

l 1 = 24.0 + ¯ 0.1 cm

l 2 = 74.70 + ¯ 0.01 cm

l 2 - l 1 = 50.70 + ¯ 0.11 cm

       Δ x x = 0.11 50.70

    Δ x % = 0.11 50.70 × 100

            = 0.22%

   Δ V % = Δ f 0 % + Δ x %

            = 1.22 %

            = 1.2 %
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A galvanometer of resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is connected to a battery of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> along with a resistance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2950</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> in series. A full-scale deflection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn></math> divisions is obtained in the galvanometer. In order to reduce this deflection to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> divisions, the resistance in series should be
questions.solution:
Current through the galvanometer

I=3(50+2950)=10-3 A

Current for 30 divisions =10-3 A

Current for 20 divisions

=10-330×20=23×10-3 A

 

For the same deflection to obtain for 20 divisions, let resistance added be R

∴ 23×10-3=350+R 

or   R=4450 Ω
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The length of a magnet is large compared to its width and breadth. The time period of its oscillation in a vibration magnetometer is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. The magnent is cut into three equal parts of length <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mi>L</mi> <mn>3</mn> </mfrac> </mrow> </math> each,and three parts are then placed on each other with their like poles together. The time period of this combination will be
questions.solution:
The time period of oscillations of magnet

T=2πIMBH                     …i

Where I= moment of inertia of magnet

=mL212 (m, being the mass of magnet)

Now magnet is cut into three equal parts.

When the three equal parts of magnet are placed on one another with their like poles together, then

           I′=112m3×L32×3

               =112mL29=I9

and M′= pole strength ×L3×3=M

Hence,

⇒        T′=13×T

             T′=23 s
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
